title: Vision-OPD: Learning to See Fine Details for Multimodal LLMs via On-Policy Self-Distillation
core_claim: A multimodal LLM can improve fine-grained visual understanding by self-distilling from its crop-conditioned predictions to its full-image predictions using on-policy rollouts, without external labels, teachers, or inference-time tools.
triage_rationale: The paper appears technically coherent and methodologically specific, with a clear self-supervised distillation setup and evaluation across multiple fine-grained vision benchmarks. It is a worthwhile review candidate because it targets a real failure mode in MLLMs and claims competitive results against stronger baselines, but the contribution is still relatively incremental and benchmark-driven rather than obviously field-defining.
likely_red_flags: Benchmark-centric claim with no external evidence of real-world robustness
Competitive-against-larger-models language may reflect a narrow evaluation setup
Novelty may be moderate if the main gain comes from leveraging crop-conditioned self-supervision
